Refactor the wl-harmonograph shapes module to use a proper trait instead of an enum + dispatch macro.

## What to do

### 1. Rewrite `src/shapes/mod.rs`

Replace the entire file with:
- A `Shape` trait with methods: `name(&self) -> &'static str`, `step(&mut self) -> Option<(f64, f64)>`, `randomize(&mut self)`, `reset(&mut self)`, `get_param(&self, name: &str) -> Option<f64>`, `set_param(&mut self, name: &str, value: f64) -> bool`, `all_params(&self) -> Vec<(&'static str, f64)>`
- Keep `SHAPE_NAMES` as a const array (same order as before)
- A `shape_from_name(name: &str) -> Option<Box<dyn Shape>>` function (replaces `Shape::from_name`)
- A `random_shape() -> Box<dyn Shape>` function (replaces `Shape::random()`)
- A `next_shape_name(current: &str) -> &'static str` function (replaces the old `Shape::next_name`)
- Keep the `CurveDrawer` struct but change its `shape` field from the old `Shape` enum to `Box<dyn Shape>`
- DELETE the old `Shape` enum and the `dispatch!` macro entirely
- Keep the same public module declarations (`pub mod harmonograph;` etc)
- Keep the same use statements for importing the shape structs

The CurveDrawer methods stay mostly the same. Update:
- `new()` takes `Box<dyn Shape>` 
- `randomize_new_shape()` uses `random_shape()`
- `switch_shape()` uses `shape_from_name()`
- Remove the `#[allow(dead_code)]` annotations â€” keep `randomize()` and `new_random()` since they're useful API surface, but remove `get_param` delegation since it's dead code. Actually just keep them all without the allow annotations since they're part of the public API.

### 2. Update all 15 shape files

For each shape file (harmonograph.rs, spirograph.rs, lissajous.rs, rose.rs, butterfly.rs, lorenz.rs, wireframe.rs, torusknot.rs, clifford.rs, dejong.rs, superformula.rs, guilloche.rs, dopendulum.rs, rossler.rs, surface.rs):

- Add `use super::Shape;` at the top (after existing imports)
- Split the `impl ShapeName` block into two: 
  - Keep `new()` and any private helper methods in `impl ShapeName { ... }`
  - Move these 7 methods into `impl Shape for ShapeName { ... }`: `name`, `step`, `randomize`, `reset`, `get_param`, `set_param`, `all_params`
  - Change `name()` signature from `pub fn name() -> &'static str` to `fn name(&self) -> &'static str` (add &self, remove pub)
  - Change all other trait methods from `pub fn` to `fn` (trait methods don't need pub)

### 3. Update `src/ascii.rs`

Change the import from `use crate::shapes::{Shape, SHAPE_NAMES};` to `use crate::shapes::{self, Shape, SHAPE_NAMES};`

In the `AsciiApp` struct, change field `shape: Shape` to `shape: Box<dyn Shape>`.

Update these calls:
- `Shape::random()` â†’ `shapes::random_shape()`
- `Shape::from_name(name)` â†’ `shapes::shape_from_name(name)` (returns Option<Box<dyn Shape>>)
- `self.shape.next_name()` â†’ `shapes::next_shape_name(self.shape.name())`
- `Shape::from_name(&name).unwrap_or_else(Shape::random)` â†’ `shapes::shape_from_name(&name).unwrap_or_else(shapes::random_shape)`

### 4. Update `src/main.rs`

Change the import line:
`use wl_harmonograph::shapes::{self, CurveDrawer};` (remove the old `CurveDrawer` import path since it's now just `shapes::CurveDrawer`)

Wait, check the current import. It should be: `use wl_harmonograph::shapes::{self, CurveDrawer};`

Update these calls in main.rs:
- `shapes::Shape::random()` â†’ `shapes::random_shape()`
- `shapes::Shape::from_name(name)` â†’ `shapes::shape_from_name(name)` 
- `self.curve.shape.next_name()` â†’ `shapes::next_shape_name(self.curve.shape.name())`
- `initial_shape.name()` still works since Box<dyn Shape> has .name()

The `CurveDrawer::new(initial_shape)` call stays the same since it now takes Box<dyn Shape>.

IMPORTANT: Read each file completely before editing. Use the Edit tool (find exact text, replace) for surgical changes. Do NOT use Write to overwrite entire files â€” use Edit for each specific change.

After all edits, run `cargo check` to verify everything compiles. Fix any errors.
